sous_titres.text: Trying to freeze me and the kid before we get to town, Marshall?
It's colder than the balls of hell in here.
- The fuck you say? - Sit the fuck down, Floyd.
- You wanna let me out of these shackles?
Jesus, Deputy, he's just a kid.
I won't hesitate to shoot you.
Hey, are you really Calamity Jane?
And any other valuables you may be carrying
- Okay, on the count three. - What? Wha--
I am the Deputy Sheriff in the city of Deadwood,
transportin' these prisoners back to face justice.
I'm afraid we're at an impasse.
- What are you doin'? - Well, I appreciate the help,
but that don't change the fact you got a date with the judge.
You need to get that kid a doctor.
I need to get you both in that cell.
In a land built on broken oaths?
He'll get a fair trial if he lasts that long.
if I were you, I'd be a little more concerned
It'd be a shame if anything happened to them.
Yeah, from what I hear, your sheriff ain't much for following the law.
Rumor has it, he likes being judge and executioner.
What about me saving your ass?
You think saving a couple lives absolves you of your sins?
No. Nor do I give a buffalo shit.
I'm wanted for murder in five states.
He says they gonna hang me in the morning.
Well I tell you a little secret?
Ain't the first time that they try.
I ain't yet done leaving my mark.
My brother's coming tomorrow to bust me out.
I might let you come with us if you ask nice.
You best not piss me off,
Yeah, that's right. Take a nice look.
You're exactly the firestorm I imagined, Calamity Jane.
You mean ain't nobody gonna read your letter?
It's not a letter. It's a book.
I was covering the war for Harper's Weekly
until the killing got to me.
Family's torn apart, fighting one another,
So I headed out West and here I am.
So how'd you get the name Calamity Jane?
I'm guessing your mama didn't call you that.
Now, Deputy, you're not thinking about putting little old me
in your book now, are you?
Mama died of pneumonia when I was 12.
Pa two years later, leaving me to raise the family.
You're gonna wanna write this down.
Oh, well, is it-- is it story time now?
Sure is. And I'll be sure to use small words for you.
That name was given to me by good old Cap Egan
when we was fighting Indians just south of Goose Creek.
They ambushed us about a mile from our post.
Cap Egan takes an arrow and nearly dies twice falling off his steed,
and I snatched him up before they could maul him and took one in the gut.
When we got back to camp,
he got the whole regiment together,
To Calamity Jane, Queen of the High Plain.
Memory gets a little murky on account of the whiskey.
Hurts worse than a conversation with a priest when it rains.
You ever get tired of it?
Imagine a lot comes along with a name like Calamity.
But now, history doesn't remember Janes.
coach full of arrows tells me that I'm in the right place.
- Can I help you, sir? - Backin' the fuck up
Well, if it ain't Wild Bill himself.
Ooh, you guys have a collection
of real nasty folks here, don't we?
Got no quarrel with you as of yet.
I'm just here to see my wife-to-be.
Heard the coach got shot up. Just wanted to make sure that you were--
as I can be under the circumstances.
Aren't you gonna put it on?
I got something else for you.
that little plot of land outside Lewis County?
And the orchard with the little apple trees.
Wild Bill Hickok's just gonna settle down and become a fuckin' farmer?
That's exactly what he's gonna do, with you.
Maybe a few little ones running around.
Now, what trivial matter did you arrest my wife-to-be for?
She got drunk and robbed some ranchers.
They were thieving wrestlers. They besmirched my honor,
Well, I can still smell the whiskey.
I'll talk to the bellman in the mornin'.
We'll have you outta here 'bout midday.
When the times are getting dark,
and the night things are growling at you,
remember to lean on the good work of the Lord for guidance.
Nothing wrong with spreading the word of the Lord.
we don't see too much of the good Lord here in Deadwood.
Promise you'll go straight to sleep and wait for the magistrate.
No fuckin' cards, Bill, I mean it.
I heard you. High noon tomorrow.
What are you doin' with that killer?
You and I did some killing earlier ourselves, Deputy, or did you forget that already?
That was different. We was defending ourselves.
There is a darkness in his eyes.
You ain't got what he got.
You don't know shit about me.
He just needs a little salvation is all.
There ain't nothing waiting in the sky
the good Lord sent his favorite son
Ain't no room for you at this table, Wild Bill.
still making his living off the backs of others?
If you know my brother, you know he don't take kindly
Yeah, I reckon I don't give a fuck.
I'm afraid my man Riley's sitting there.
You're fuckin' crazy, just like they say.
What's the most you ever lost on a hand?
What's the most you ever lost on a hand?
has been trained at your nuts since I sat down.
Now, from what I can gather,
And if it ain't, I don't think I'd trust it in a gunfight.
Now your bullet could go to the right, could go to the left.
That thing could backfire, hit you right in the face, which from where I'm sitting,
well, that'd be a fuckin' improvement.
Only man here allowed to dirty these floors
Well, since the lady says so,
and since I'm in such a good mood...
Jack, Jack, Jack, you-- you're forgetting something.
You-you forgot to say thank you.
- What? - You forgot to say thank you.
Thank you for letting me live.
Oh, I'm just fuckin' with you. Thank you.
As an outlaw, what would you say you fear the most?
I'd say dumb fuckin' questions are at the top of the list.
Just out for a swim, were you?
A hundred miles working for General Crook.
Doc said I collapsed and died.
Fourteen days later, I wake up in the hospital.
Is that what you're gonna put in your book? Hmm?
Miracle's just another word for dumb luck.
You honestly think it'd make a difference if I did?
Jack McCall blew his fuckin' brains out
The undertaker's got his body already.
Now, I'm gonna go make sure he doesn't skip town.
You stay here and keep a look out.
- Are you sure you don't need-- - Stay here.
Rick knows Jack McCall killed Wild Bill Hickok?
Wild Bill shot my friend's uncle
outside of Promise, Nevada, without no provoking.
He got what was coming to him.
Maybe that's just God's way of evening things out.
I wish it were me that had done it.
the man who killed Wild Bill?
than a Texas tornado, don't she?
Now, when you put this part
in your little book, make sure you get my name right.
You're a thoroughbred, aren't you, lawman?
Do you know where maybe I could find him?
Last time he came through here, he stayed at the Stone Inn.
How about I come and see you tonight?
It's gonna be real busy at the saloon.
I left you ten minutes ago.
Careful now. You're about to get in bed with the man
who killed frontier legend Wild Bill Hickok.
- I got money. - You think this is about money?
- Get outta my way! - You gonna execute me
- like you did Sheriff Griggs? - Jack shot Griggs.
Step aside, Deputy. He dies, here and now.
He'll stand trial and he'll be hung.
Just let me help you. Please, just put down the gun.
I gotta take you back in.
You say you didn't kill the sheriff?
Well, you can tell that to the judge.
- Where'd she go? - That way, that way!
Oh, whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa.
Hey, look who it is. We came to break you out.
Sorry to ruin it for you, baby brother. Waited as long as I could.
What the hell are we supposed to do now?
Well, first I'm gonna get me a drink
and then I'm sure we'll figure something out, won't we?
Surveyor told me this place belongs to Wild Bill Hickok.
Ran into a couple ranchers this morning.
Said they saw you heading up this way.
Make a noise and your dead
Do I need to worry about you
trying to stuff me in a box?
I don't do the killings around here.
Oh, you cleaned him up real nice.
Uh, we burned 'em. That's-- that's--that's tradition.
Did he have anything on him?
No, ma'am. Not by the time he got to me, he didn't.
Can you give us a moment?
Can you do me a favor, gravedigger?
that he was a decent man?
That's got to account for something, right?
May I ask who's makin' the request?
You know, I've been thinking about what I'd do
if I had found myself a gold mine.
Well, I figured first thing I'd do is,
I'd buy myself a brand-new Winchester,
and a whole goddamn lot of them fancy Scottish cattle
If you had a gold mine, I'd kill you and take all your gold.
That's not what I was saying.
West's all one big rotten fuckin' drain.
Uh, he said he was coming, yeah.
And he said he was gonna pay us 400 a piece
to take him to see his brother?
If it ain't Jack McCall himself.
This is the man you was talking about?
You're fixing to pay us 500 a piece
to take you to see your brother?
and need some protection, is all.
If you got the gold, we are.
There's a thousand in gold right there.
My brother will give you the rest when you take me to him.
Put 'em down. We leave at first light.
And I swear to God, brother, if you play the harmonica one more time,
a man wiser than me once said,
if you go to embark on a quest for revenge,
One for them and one for yourself.
That just sounds like a lot of fuckin' digging.
You even know where he's headed?
I hear his brother runs a bunch of gold mines out east.
Well, the only way to reach those gold mines out east
is to travel through the Badlands.
that make the savages look like English royalty.
Why do I get the funny feeling you might know another way?
- Whatever you think's fair. - How does it work?
We just go right on through.
Right on through the ex-Confederate devils
that make the savages look like English royalty?
I gotta go through there to pick up the product.
I really am gonna kill Jack McCall,
and you better not get in my way.
You pull the trigger, I'll build the coffin.
Calamity Jane is wanted for the death of Sheriff Griggs.
Jack McCall is wanted for the death of Wild Bill Hickok.
and one of them our own.
Hell of a night, even for Deadwood.
This is a hunt. We are bringing them back to the hangman.
If we don't kill them ourselves, well we don't let 'em go either.
We bring 'em back here for justice.
As the acting sheriff, I have been authorized
to deputize you men to carry out the law of this land.
Will nobody here stand for Sheriff Briggs?
You barely look old enough to drink.
You know how to wield an iron?
No age requirement on doing what's right.
You know how to use that thing?
I've been shooting since I was nine.
Three of y'all. That's your posse?
I saw Calamity Jane. She's with the undertaker,
and they're headed for the Badlands.
Well, get some shut-eye. We ride at first light.
Find it helps keep the devil at bay,
The only thing that's real is pain.
I hate to be the bearer of bad news,
but there's a long fucking line of people ahead of you
with a bone to pick with the Almighty.
What'd you lose that's got you so pissed off at God?
The fire's a lot less merciful than a bullet.
Even if you get your revenge,
you're gonna be dealing with the pain.
You can trust me on that.
It's the way of the world, really.
Looks like it's time to play dead.
Got a body to deliver to Willow Creek.
Gun or the gold. What'll it be?
What do you think a scalp like that will fetch in market, boys?
I mean, it ain't fuckin' enough.
Rusty said it was two pieces.
Shit, I don't think he can hear much of anything anymore.
Look, all I'm fixing to do is deliver a man to his loved ones.
You're lucky we're out here looking out for you.
You need roughnecks like us out here
filled with nothing but fuckin' savages.
You got a live one back there?
Let's take a look at that fancy woodwork.
- I'm trying to stop the-- - Okay, okay.
Just let me enjoy the stars.
I shouldn't have brought you here.
I always thought when this moment came,
All we can do is try, you know?
when the end comes at some point
maybe if you're lucky, the good outweighs the bad.
You know, they say I swam 100 miles in freezing river?
I, uh, I never swam the Platte.
Technically, I did, because I fell in drunk, damn near drowned.
Well, I mean, you still got time.
From what I heard though, it's pretty fuckin' cold.
I shoot you, it's your brains all over my pretty face.
You got Jack McCall in there?
You gonna give him to me?
- Why? - Well, for one thing, attitude.
He has gold. Lots and lots of gold.
Well, you know, some things are more important than gold.
Hands where I can see them.
We're here for the outlaw, Jack McCall...
Both wanted for murder. Nobody has to die here today.
A shot like that, Wild Bill'd still be alive.
You kill him, you hang for sure.
There's two types of people, Jack!
Harper, we'll get them from behind.
Take me to my fuckin' brother.
Get your hands off me. Get up.
Killed a lot of people in my time, never claimed that I didn't kill one that I did.
How'd that bullet taste, shit bag?
- What happened? - Three of them took off, on horseback.
We got one pinned in here.
You gonna make us come all the way up there and get ya?
Taking you in, dead or alive.
No one takes a Hollan brother alive.
- Where's Jack? - I don't know where Jack is.
'Cause that feels like information you might have shared.
I keep digging around in there, and gangrenes gonna get it.
Even if you live, somebody's gonna have to lop the fucker off.
Okay, okay, okay! Okay, they headed just north of here.
God! God! God, I'll tell you! I'll tell you on the map.
I'll tell you on the map.
Jesus Christ. Just... Just stop. Just stop.
Can you tell me what's taking you so long to write that book?
An outlaw tryin' to do right.
Yeah. Luck ain't got nothing to do with it.
Maybe your problem is that everybody's got
some good and evil in him.
I ain't gotta worry about you tomorrow, do I?
Bring back Jack in handcuffs, not a coffin.
What if it was your wife who he shot in the back?
- I ain't got a wife. - Mm.
Seven years ago when I was away coverin' the war for Harper's,
gang of bandits ambushed our town.
That's when I gave up writing and took off for the law.
You didn't think about riding out and killing him?
All I found was the bottom of a bottle.
Is that why you picked up the badge?
I was drinking myself to death when I found myself in debt.
I woke up one morning staring straight into the eyes of a killer.
I must have pissed off someone.
Right there and then, I decided it was a sign from my wife.
Now why the hell you go and say a thing like that? Hmm?
- We make our own luck. - Mm-hmm.
Mine's not far. Probably up there.
You're gonna have to make me.
What are we waitin' for? Stop wastin' time.
Let's hear what these good folk have to say
before we go about ending anyone's days.
You will do nothin' of the sort.
how can we help you, Sheriff?
that murderin' son of a bitch.
He is a son of a bitch.

which makes this particular murderin' piece of shit my brother.

Well, now, it appears we're at a bit of an impasse.

He needs to stand trial for what he did.

You see, Jack here, ahem, tends to our Mama's grave
I have never been able to fuckin' fathom.

I'll hold their fire. You head for the horses.
She got more fight in her than all you spineless dogs!

I could use someone like you.

with a Homestead Act magistrate comes in handy.

we dance with savages and wallows.
I'll tell you what's gonna happen.
I'm gonna take my deed back,
And then I'm gonna dance on your fucking grave.
You shot her husband in the back.
I want some time with her.
Gotta get that gold over that mountain before snowfall.
This is why we came out west.
But that just means more share for us.
We got ourselves one hell of a haul here, boys.
Uncle Sam knows it's out here, same as us.
So that means we gotta take it quick as we can.
Well, what are you waiting for?
Oh, and please, mind the dynamite.
You know, you and I, we're the same.
You stabbed your own fuckin' brother.
I didn't make you do shit.
You and I could've been friends.
You're too good, is that it?
You ever shut the fuck up?
it's gonna be forgotten like a speck of dust in the desert.
But it's gonna live on right here.
You see, you're gonna be the one adding to my legend...
Shut up or kill me, you fuckin' imbecile.

fat and happy on a beautiful ranch
at the edge of some God-forsaken little town.
I only ever told you the truth.

Stand up. Come on, there you go.
Tell me what your name is.
I grew up with four brothers,
and I got three left on account of you.

No, I didn't touch your brother, I...
I just fucked up his leg a little.
I might let you keep one of your eyes.
That way you can still make a decent wage
You deserve your revenge. I get that.
But there's something you should know about me.
I can't work at a whore house.
You gotta be fuckin' kidding me.
I think I'm the real Calamity.
and the creature that's lurking inside of you.

and it's gonna tear you fuckin' apart!

I want this all packed and loaded

I'm here to arrest Jack McCall,

I like the odds on my side.

If I die, I'm taking you with me.

is what you took from me.

'Cause if you don't, I'll be coming back.

just being Jane for a while.

I'll be on the lookout for that book of yours.
